name: United Arab Emirates
contextBlurb: On the UAE mainland, retail payments are permitted only with CBUAE-approved Payment Tokens (fiat-referenced stablecoins), notably Dirham Payment Tokens (DPTs). Use of other crypto for general merchant checkout is prohibited. Foreign Payment Tokens (FPTs) are limited to narrow use-cases (e.g., paying for virtual assets). The Central Bank’s Payment Token Services Regulation (PTSR) is in force; Dubai’s VARA regulates virtual-asset activity in Dubai (ex-DIFC) and defers AED-referenced FRVAs to CBUAE. ADGM/FSRA has an FRT (fiat-referenced token) regime in consultation.

legislation.title: ADGM FSRA consults on expanding fiat-referenced token rules (CP9/2025) to cover custody, intermediation and acceptance services.
Merchant transition period ends – UAE retailers outside free zones must now accept only licensed dirham payment tokens.
ADQ, FAB and IHC publicly launch the UAE’s first sovereign dirham-backed stablecoin on ADI blockchain, regulated by CBUAE.

news.headline: The New CBUAE Payment Token Services Regulation: 10 Things You Need to Know

stablecoinMeta.summaryEn: On the UAE mainland, retail payments are permitted only with CBUAE-approved Payment Tokens (fiat-referenced stablecoins), notably Dirham Payment Tokens (DPTs). Use of other crypto for general merchant checkout is prohibited. Foreign Payment Tokens (FPTs) are limited to narrow use-cases (e.g., paying for virtual assets). The Central Bank’s Payment Token Services Regulation (PTSR) is in force; Dubai’s VARA regulates virtual-asset activity in Dubai (ex-DIFC) and defers AED-referenced FRVAs to CBUAE. ADGM/FSRA has an FRT (fiat-referenced token) regime in consultation.
stablecoinMeta.classificationNote: UAE law classifies stablecoins as “Payment Tokens” under CBUAE regulation – digital representations of fiat currency used for payment and settlement, distinct from securities or commodities.

regulators.name: Central Bank of the UAE
regulators.role: Issues Payment Token Services Regulation; licenses dirham-backed stablecoins and supervises payment-token service providers.

regulators.name: Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority (VARA) – Dubai
regulators.role: Regulates virtual-asset service providers in Dubai (excluding DIFC); oversees issuance and trading of fiat-referenced virtual assets.
